title: Polish editorial-pass notes
description: Output of the /polish Phase 3 (editorial reduction) editor subagent: a structured critique the rewriter subagent then applies. The editor never rewrites — it only emits this file.

doc_format.description: The source document's format, carried from Phase 1.

target.description: The reduction target the user picked (preset range, custom range/point, or the open-ended ~50%+ which is a point of 50).

reconciliation.description: Editor's self-assessment: does the sum of est_words_saved reach the target band without touching protected content or destroying meaning?

recritique.description: Set after the (single, capped) re-critique pass per GR-3. Absent on the first editor pass.

notes.description: One entry per reduction opportunity. The rewriter applies risk=low notes; risk=high notes are surfaced to the user via the findings protocol.

id.description: Sequential note id, e.g. E1, E2, ... (re-critique appends with continuing ids).

kind.description: What the rewriter should do. 'reorder' is always risk=high; a 'merge' spanning >2 adjacent paragraphs is risk=high.

locator.description: How the rewriter finds the span. No line numbers — they go stale.

heading_path.description: Enclosing headings (markdown # levels or HTML <hN> text), outermost first.

quote.description: A verbatim substring of the source document (>= 20 chars). cut/tighten notes whose quote is empty or not a verbatim substring are dropped before the rewriter runs.

rationale.description: Why this edit. Surfaced to the user for risk=high notes.

est_words_saved.description: Estimated words removed by this note. May be 0 for a pure 'rephrase'.

risk.description: low → rewriter applies it; high → surfaced for approval (structural reorders always surfaced individually).
